# Requirements: FlawChess — v1.24 Library Page (ARCHIVED)

**Status:** ✅ SHIPPED 2026-06-09 — all 18 requirements Complete.
**Defined:** 2026-06-05 · **Archived:** 2026-06-09 at milestone close.
**Core Value:** Position-precise WDL across openings + endgames + time pressure on top of users' actual chess.com / lichess games, with personalized LLM commentary on endgame performance and an auto-generated opening-strengths/weaknesses report.

**Scope note (how this milestone grew):** Requirements were defined on 2026-06-05 covering only the Library shell (LIB-01..09, Phase 104) plus the analysis-half backend + first frontend surfaces (LIBG-01..03, LIBG-06..10, Phases 105–107, 109). The milestone then expanded through roadmap evolution into a full eval-driven mistake/flaw archive: **Phase 108** (Flaws subtab + `game_flaws` materialization + cross-tab Flaw filter, from SEED-038), **Phase 110** (flaw-tag taxonomy overhaul), **Phase 111** (Library UI polish — staged Apply-only filter model; shipped via direct commits, no requirement ID), and **Phase 112** (Flaws subtab card rework). Those four phases carry no LIB/LIBG requirement IDs — they were driven by SEED-036 / SEED-038 and locked `/gsd-explore` decisions rather than the original requirement set. The deferred SEED-036 surfaces (LIBG-04 Analysis viewer, LIBG-05 best-move endpoint) remain unbuilt and stay in `.planning/seeds/SEED-036-library-page-milestone.md`.

## v1 Requirements — Library Page (Phase 104)

- [x] **LIB-01**: User can navigate to a new top-level **Library** page from the desktop nav, the mobile bottom bar, and the mobile "More" drawer.
- [x] **LIB-02**: The Library page presents deep-linkable, URL-routed subtabs using the same Openings-style `<Tabs variant="brand">` pattern (`navigate('/library/<tab>')`), with each subtab implemented as its own `.tsx` file mirroring the Openings page structure.
- [x] **LIB-03**: User can access the existing Import workflow as the **Import** subtab at `/library/import` (its own tsx, leftmost subtab); visiting the old `/import` URL redirects to `/library/import`.
- [x] **LIB-04**: User can access the existing global Overview dashboard as the **Overview** subtab at `/library/overview` (its own tsx, migrated from `GlobalStats.tsx`); visiting the old `/overview` URL redirects to `/library/overview`.
- [x] **LIB-05**: Top-level navigation shows **Library · Openings · Endgames** (plus the superuser-only Admin item), with Import and Overview removed as standalone top-level nav items.
- [x] **LIB-06**: The `totalGames === 0` notification dot appears on the **Library** nav item (moved from the former Import nav item).
- [x] **LIB-07**: The default landing subtab is state-dependent — a user with zero imported games lands on **Import**; a user with games lands on **Overview** (and, once the Games subtab shipped in Phase 107, on **Games**). Home's gameless-user redirect targets `/library/import`.
- [x] **LIB-08**: The Library page is **always accessible** (never import-gated, because it hosts Import); the Import and Overview subtabs are always open.
- [x] **LIB-09**: The Library page and both subtabs work on mobile — responsive subtab control plus the responsive layouts preserved from the migrated Import and Overview pages, with `data-testid` / ARIA / semantic-HTML conventions on all new interactive elements and containers.

## Analysis-half Requirements — Games & Mistakes (Phases 105–109)

- [x] **LIBG-01** *(Phase 107)*: Games subtab frontend — filterable game-card archive (existing metadata filters + the boolean mistake-severity filter; no chessboard, no opening filter), cards reusing the Openings game-card with per-game B/M/I counts + curated/deduped family-colored tag chips + explicit "no engine analysis" state, on the Openings mobile-drawer pattern. Consumes `GET /api/library/games`.
- [x] **LIBG-02** *(Phase 105)*: On-the-fly mistake-detection service deriving per-ply severity (inaccuracy/mistake/blunder) from stored `eval_cp` / `eval_mate`, using the Lichess-aligned expected-score-drop thresholds (0.05 / 0.10 / 0.15 on the [0,1] ES scale), from the mover's perspective; mate handled via the ±1000 cp-equivalent mapping (Option B); chess.com / unanalyzed games yield an explicit "no analysis" result.
- [x] **LIBG-03** *(Phase 107)*: Flaw-Stats panel (frontend) — per-severity counts/rates per mistake type (normalized per game and per 100 moves), tag distribution (tempo split, impact rate, phase histogram), trend over time, and the explicit `% analyzed` (≥90%-coverage) denominator + N.
- [ ] **LIBG-04**: Analysis subtab — per-flaw viewer (board, stepper, move list, eval/material timeline, jump-to-mistake), deep-link detail route. *(DEFERRED — later phase; stays in SEED-036)*
- [ ] **LIBG-05**: On-demand single-position best-move endpoint (`POST /api/analysis/best-move`), rate-limited / concurrency-capped. *(DEFERRED — later phase; stays in SEED-036)*
- [x] **LIBG-06** *(Phase 105)*: The detection service emits the per-flaw attribution tags — `miss`, `unpunished`, the impact family, `phase`, and exactly one tempo tag (move-time + clock derived; thresholds documented and tunable). *(Tag taxonomy subsequently finalized in Phase 110: tempo `hasty`/`unrushed`, impact ladder `reversed`/`squandered`.)*
- [x] **LIBG-07** *(Phase 105)*: Each flaw is returned as a typed structured object (ply, FEN, side, severity, tags, eval before/after) documented as the consumption contract for the Games / Flaws / Analysis surfaces and SEED-037 Train; materialization landed as a drop-in optimization in Phase 108 (`game_flaws`).
- [x] **LIBG-08** *(Phase 106)*: Games-list endpoint extends `apply_game_filters` with a boolean mistake-type `EXISTS` filter and returns per-game B/M/I severity counts plus a curated, aggregated/deduped set of card tag-chips, reusing the Phase 105 kernel; chess.com / unanalyzed-lichess games return an explicit "no engine analysis" state. *(Migrated off the on-the-fly window-scan onto `game_flaws` in Phase 108.)*
- [x] **LIBG-09** *(Phase 106)*: Stats-panel aggregate endpoint over the filtered analyzed-only set — per-severity counts/rates (per game + per 100 moves), the full tag distribution, and a trend-over-time series — with the explicit `% analyzed` (≥90%-per-ply-coverage) denominator and analyzed-game N.
- [x] **LIBG-10** *(Phase 109)*: Per-card expected-score eval chart on the Games subtab — each *analyzed* card shows a recharts area chart of white-perspective expected score per ply (lichess sigmoid, 50% midline, advantage region shaded), rendered as a dedicated middle column (three equal thirds) with mobile parity. Dots mark the user's flaws only; vertical lines mark opening / middlegame / endgame transitions; per-ply tooltips show eval in pawns (or mate in #N) plus flaw tags. Delivered inline by extending the existing `GET /api/library/games` payload (no new endpoint, no migration).

## Requirement-less phases (roadmap-evolution scope)

| Phase | Driver | What shipped |
|-------|--------|--------------|
| 108 | SEED-038 + `/gsd-discuss-phase` | Flaws subtab, `game_flaws` materialization table + Alembic migration, per-flaw list endpoint, shared cross-tab Flaw filter, `scripts/backfill_flaws.py` |
| 110 | `.planning/notes/flaw-tag-definitions.md` | Flaw-tag taxonomy overhaul — tempo rename (`hasty`/`unrushed`), impact ladder (`reversed`/`squandered`), canonical chip names + definition popovers, active-filter highlight |
| 111 | `/gsd-explore` polish pass | Library UI polish — staged Apply-only filter model, FilterActions footer, Tags panel rename (shipped via direct commits; no GSD plan/summary artifacts retained) |
| 112 | `/gsd-explore` 2026-06-09 | Flaws subtab card rework — 2-up `Card` grid, banded header + 132px miniboard + eval swing, `GET /api/library/games/{game_id}` single-game endpoint, View-game modal |
